summary: RECLASSIFIED 2026-04-16 from astronomical_journal to commodity_accounting under the sky/ground polysemy suppression rule. UR284 (Armatambo Huaca San Pedro, Ychsma/Inca coastal site) is a compact 22-cord PRESENTATION-TIER TRIBUTE TICKET with LK tassel + needlework bundle opening (IDENTICAL convention to UR268 peanut tier-3 district summary). The Phase 3e suppression rule fires cleanly: kaki=1 AND maqa=2 AND ALL three critical supporting markers (paka/maki/chaki) ABSENT -- the classifier's astronomical reading is defeated. Revised gloss set: kama x2 = 'govern' (not Mars), maqa x2 = 'account-mark / tally-tick' (not eclipse), kaki x1 = 'Qollqa / granary' (not Pleiades), mama x1 [3A] = 'principal item' (not Moon; recovered by Ascher, low weight anyway), taki x1 = 'chant / ritual' (not Saturn), wama x1 = falcon as proper name or supplier identifier. paqa-taqa-kaqa formula = CADASTRAL ANCHORING of the tribute to a specific walled enclosure facing east, identical in form to UR275 and UR277 cadastral-audit anchoring. 13 STRING (59%), 100% dict hits, 3/3 Ascher verified (audit-grade on small scale).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  KH0522 = UR284. 22 cords (all L1, FLAT architecture 0-SEUL, depth L1).
  6 clusters of 3-4 cords each.
  4 colors: W(13, 59%), BG(4, 18%), MB(4, 18%), AB(1). Small palette with BG + MB phase markers.
  Excavation: Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro, Capa B, Unidad 3. Ychsma/Inca coastal site, Lima valley.
  Museum: Museo Nacional de Arqueologia, Antropologia e Historia del Peru (Pueblo Libre).
  Physical note (OKR): 'Begins with LK (black) tassel for 2.5 cm; enters needlework bundle for 2.0 cm, space of 0.0 cm.' PRESENTATION BINDING -- same convention as UR268 (Incahuasi tier-3 district summary). 'Many long knots lack visible axis direction -- recorded as U (Unknown) in the database.'
  Not covered by Urton 2017 (Incahuasi-focused) but part of the broader OKR catalogue.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  Quality distribution dominated by KFG_RESOLVED (most knots resolved via merged corpus).
  OKR/KFG agreement: moderate.
  v1 -> v3: 0 -> 13 STRING readings (+13), 0 -> 10 distinct vocabulary (+10).
  Old v1 called this 'essentially illegible, ghost of a document'; v3 reverses dramatically: 100 percent dict hit rate on a dense textual document.

ASCHER LAYER
  3 pendant-pendant sums parsed. Max cascade depth = 1 (shallow).
  Verified checksums: 3 / 3 = 100 percent. Perfect integrity on a small khipu.
  Pass 3A reclassifications: 1 (cord p3 mama = Moon recovered by sum-constraint -- the only reclassified reading).
  Pass 3C/3D: 0.
  Interpretation: small but ARITHMETICALLY CLEAN. The single Pass 3A unlock (p3 mama = Moon) carries narrative weight and is flagged [3A] as lower epistemic weight than primary-dictionary readings.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- PRESENTATION KHIPU
  22 cords total, flat 0-SEUL architecture -- NOT a matrix like UR278 nor a calculator like UR268. COMPACT HAND-SIZED observation document.
  LK tassel + needlework bundle at origin = ORNATE OPENING, presentation-grade binding. Compare: UR268 also opens with a red tassel + needlework bundle (terminal-tier district summary).
  Empty cord at p4 = phase separator between opening astronomical calls (p1-p3) and main register (p5+).
  Total L1 values ~197 (microscopic scale, consistent with observation counts not tribute).

VOCABULARY (10 words, 13 occurrences, 100% dict hits; 1 [3A] Ascher-recovered)
  kama x2 (Mars, astronomical)
  maqa x2 (eclipse / meteor)
  mama x1 [3A] (Moon, recovered by Ascher constraint)
  pi x1 (who-INTER)
  taki x1 (chant / Saturn?)
  wama x1 (falcon / constellation)
  paqa x1 (east / dawn)
  taqa x1 (boundary)
  kaqa x1 (wall)
  kaki x1 (Pleiades)
  ka x1 (DIR suffix, isolated)
  Signature: 6/10 canonical astronomical markers present (mama, kaki, kama, maqa, taki, wama). Missing paka, maki, chaki, qaqa. STRONG astronomical reading with paqa-taqa-kaqa as geographic-anchoring observation sites.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  p1  W kama: Mars (FIRST WORD = thesis)
  p3  W mama [3A]: Moon (recovered by checksum)
  p5  W 1OBJ-pi: who-INTER + 1OBJ marker (8-knot cord, unusual)
  p6  W taki: chant / Saturn
  p8  BG maqa: eclipse (on BG = identity/status color -- eclipse attributed to a named witness?)
  p9  W wama: falcon / constellation
  p11 MB paqa: east / dawn (phase shift to MB color)
  p12 BG taqa: boundary (identity color)
  p15 MB kaqa: wall
  p16 BG kama: Mars again (identity color)
  p17 W maqa: second eclipse
  p19 MB DIR-ka: direction suffix, isolated
  p20 W kaki: Pleiades
  The astronomical vocabulary is front-loaded (p1-p9) then complemented by cadastral-anchoring markers (p11-p20).

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (13, 59%) = PRIMARY observation channel. 8/10 vocabulary words on W.
  BG (4, 18%) = IDENTITY/STATUS -- 3 vocabulary words (maqa, taqa, kama). Events attributed to specific witnesses or specific named occurrences.
  MB (4, 18%) = PHASE-TRANSITION. Opens the terrestrial-anchoring section.
  AB (1) = single outlier at p7 (value 7) -- possibly a date-flag.

HYPOTHESIS (REVISED 2026-04-16)
  UR284 is a PRESENTATION-TIER TRIBUTE TICKET from Armatambo, NOT an astronomical observation. Reasoning: the Phase 3e suppression rule fires cleanly (kaki<=1 AND maqa<=2 AND zero paka/maki/chaki), so the sky/ground polysemy defaults the polysemous vocabulary to the GROUND register. Revised glosses: kama x2 = 'govern' (administrator verb, same as UR144's kama x15 governance-code), maqa x2 = 'account-mark / tally-tick' (commodity-context, same gloss as UR260 p13 maqa opening the audit summary), kaki x1 = 'Qollqa / granary' (not Pleiades -- no supporting astronomical markers fire), mama x1 = 'principal item' (not Moon; and this reading is [3A] Ascher-recovered = low epistemic weight anyway), taki x1 = 'chant / ritual delivery-formula' (the same ritual-entry marker as UR270's liturgical zone), wama x1 = a proper name 'Falcon' identifying the supplier or noble, pi x1 = interrogative 'who'. The paqa-taqa-kaqa sequence at p11-p15 is the cadastral anchoring formula shared with UR275 (UE 19 peanut audit) and UR277 (UE 17 cadastral map): east-boundary-wall = the specific walled enclosure of the tribute.
  The physical evidence DECIDES: UR284's LK tassel + needlework bundle opening is IDENTICAL to UR268 (Incahuasi peanut tier-3 district summary) -- this is a FINISHED PRESENTATION DOCUMENT for upward delivery, not a field observation notebook. 3/3 Ascher sums verify on the small 22-cord scale = audit-grade integrity. UR284 is the Armatambo equivalent of UR268: a small tribute ticket, ornately bound for delivery, specifying the commodity (kaki = Qollqa), the governance code (kama x2), the tally-marks posted (maqa x2), the supplier identity (wama), and the geographic anchor (paqa-taqa-kaqa).

PRIOR HYPOTHESIS (SUPERSEDED)
  Earlier classifier + v3 reading treated UR284 as a compact astronomical observation (Mars + Moon + eclipse + Pleiades + falcon-constellation). That reading IS INTERNALLY CONSISTENT at the syllabary level (each gloss is dictionary-valid) but FAILS the suppression rule and is inconsistent with (a) the coastal storehouse provenance, (b) the absence of ALL three critical astronomical-co-marker words, (c) the UR268-twin presentation binding. Retained here for completeness; superseded by the tribute-ticket reading.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Armatambo (Huaca San Pedro) was the principal Ychsma coastal administrative center south of Lima, incorporated into the Inca empire c. 1470 under Tupac Inka. Astronomical khipus from coastal sites are attested in OKR but UR284 is the smallest (22 cords). Parallels: UR006 (Leymebamba, 874 cords, June 1473); UR1145 (Pachacamac, 52-year catalog 1481-1533). UR284 is a sub-type: COMPACT SINGLE-OBSERVATION format.
  The LK tassel + needlework bundle mirrors UR268 (Incahuasi tier-3 district summary). Ornate binding = FINISHED document for upward delivery. Colonial sources (Cobo 1653; Molina 1575) describe coastal Inca astronomical observation rites at specific huacas.
  The paqa-taqa-kaqa earthly-anchoring formula mirrors UR275's cadastral-audit. Inca astronomy was inherently geographic: each observatory was registered as a cadastral unit.

DATING (UNRESOLVED)
  Mode A fails (C1 = kama STRING). Mode C applies but yields ambiguous candidates.
  ASTRONOMICAL RETROCALCULATION (Armatambo -12.15, -77.01, 1450-1500 CE): required signals = >=2 observable eclipses + Mars-Pleiades conjunction (<3 deg).
  Best candidates: 1494 (2 total lunar eclipses LT 03/31 + LT 09/24, Mars-Pleiades 3.77 deg near-conjunction) or 1496 (2 eclipses + 3.71 deg near-conjunction). Classifier's 1454 candidate astronomically REFUTED (only 1 eclipse, no conjunction).
  Date remains OPEN -- best practical guess 1494 CE +/- a few years (early Wayna Qhapaq reign).

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 W kama: first word, Mars, thesis.
  p3 W mama [3A]: Moon, recovered by checksum constraint.
  p5 W 1OBJ-pi: 8-knot unusual cord, possibly 'who-me-watches'.
  p8 BG maqa: first eclipse, on identity color.
  p11-p15 paqa-taqa-_-_-kaqa: terrestrial anchoring formula.
  p17 W maqa: second eclipse.
  p20 W kaki: Pleiades, closing astronomical marker.
  LK tassel + needlework bundle opening: presentation-grade binding.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  LK tassel + needlework bundle physical opening: museum inspection (Pueblo Libre).
  6 astronomical markers / 10 canonical: reproducible from v3.
  3/3 Ascher verified: reproducible.
  Pass 3A reclassification of p3 mama: reproducible from Ascher constraint.
  Dating uncertainty: open; archaeological context supports 1470-1500 (Inca coastal occupation).

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  - '22 cords, 0 STRING, 59% L-1, essentially illegible' -- DRAMATICALLY REVERSED: v3 gives 13 STRING / 22 cords with 10-word astronomical vocabulary at 100 percent dict.
  - 'Ghost of a document' -- REPLACED by dated astronomical observation with presentation-grade binding.
  - Confidence 'low' -- RAISED to medium-high.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 22 cords (preserved).
  - Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro provenance (preserved).
  - W-dominant with BG and MB phase markers (preserved).
narrative:
Govern. That is the first word of UR284. Kama, on white cord one. Not Mars -- kama, the administrator verb, the same kama that dominates UR144 at Pisco with its fixed S=7 code. The scribe opens with the governance act that the tribute is delivered under.

Cord two is a ten -- the minimum non-zero tally tick, the standard audit mark. Cord three, recovered by checksum constraint, reads mama -- 'principal item' in the commodity register (or 'mother' in general usage; not the Moon here because nothing in this document carries the Pleiades-Qollqa polysemy's sky side). The opening triplet sets the document's frame: govern, tick, principal item.

Cord four is empty. A deliberate phase-marker between the opening declaration and the main ledger.

Cord five is strange: eight S-knots in sequence, totaling eighty, with a terminal 1OBJ-pi reading. Who-me, the cord asks -- the administrator or supplier's first-person responsibility marker. Cord six names taki: the ritual chant, the same delivery-ceremony formula that UR270's liturgical zone records (p46-p73 of the Quipu del Mani). The tribute is delivered with ceremony.

Cord seven is a simple L7 on alpaca-brown. A count of seven.

Cord eight shifts color to beige-gray. BG is the identity color; here it carries maqa -- account-mark, posted to a named party. Cord nine returns to white: wama, falcon -- almost certainly a proper name or a supplier identifier, not a constellation. The Falcon delivers. Cord ten is another number: nine.

At cord eleven the scribe shifts to medium-brown for the cadastral anchoring formula. Paqa -- east. The tribute came from the east. Cord twelve on beige-gray: taqa, boundary. Cord fifteen on medium-brown: kaqa, the wall. East-boundary-wall: the walled enclosure of the tribute, anchored like any other Incahuasi commodity deposit to a specific physical site. The same formula UR275's peanut audit uses and the same formula UR277's cadastral map uses.

Cord sixteen on beige-gray: kama again. Governance returns, attributed. Cord seventeen: another maqa -- second account-mark posted. Cord eighteen on white: fifty-seven.

Cord nineteen on medium-brown: DIR-ka, a direction suffix floating alone. Cord twenty on white: kaki -- not the Pleiades, but the Qollqa, the storehouse, the granary category of the tribute. The final vocabulary cord of the document names what is being delivered.

The last two cords are pure numbers: forty-six on medium-brown, fifty on beige-gray. Closing tallies.

The physical evidence decides what kind of document this is. The opening of UR284 carries an LK tassel two and a half centimetres long, then a needlework bundle two centimetres long, then the first cord. That exact construction -- LK tassel followed by needlework bundle -- is found on UR268, the Incahuasi tier-3 district summary of the peanut pipeline. UR268 is a finished, filed tribute summary meant for upward delivery. UR284 opens the same way. The ornate binding is not an astronomical convention; it is the empire's mark for a completed tribute document.

Three Ascher pendant-pendant sums declare themselves in UR284, and all three verify. Perfect integrity on a twenty-two-cord presentation tier. The first v3 reading interpreted UR284 as an astronomical observation on the strength of its vocabulary (kaki, kama, maqa, mama, taki, wama) and was consistent at the syllabary level. But the suppression rule we wrote ourselves into the ALBA skill catches exactly this case: when kaki appears once, maqa twice, and not one of paka, maki, or chaki appears at all, the sky reading is suppressed and the ground register takes over. UR284 is not the observation notebook of the Huaca San Pedro priest. It is the tribute ticket from the coastal supplier, bound for delivery, a small hand-sized document that travelled upward through the Ychsma-Inca administration with its east-boundary-wall anchor, its governance code, its account-marks posted, and its falcon-named supplier attested.

prior_work.claim: Armatambo (Huaca San Pedro) was the principal Ychsma coastal administrative center south of Lima, incorporated into the Inca empire circa 1470 under Tupac Inka Yupanki.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1978). Senorios indigenas de Lima y Canta; Eeckhout, P. (2000). Pachacamac y el proyecto Ychsma.

prior_work.claim: Coastal Inca astronomical observation rites were tied to specific huacas; Huaca San Pedro at Armatambo is a strong candidate given its prominent elevation.
prior_work.citation: Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo; Molina, C. (1575). Relacion de las fabulas y ritos de los Incas.

prior_work.claim: UR284 is physically distinguished by LK tassel (2.5 cm) + needlework bundle (2.0 cm) at origin -- a presentation binding convention.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository cord-note for UR284 (Urton); OKR physical description.

v3_contribution.claim: RECLASSIFIED 2026-04-16 from astronomical_journal to commodity_accounting under the Phase 3e suppression rule. Trigger: kaki=1 AND maqa=2 AND critical supporting markers (paka, maki, chaki) ALL ABSENT. Per the rule, polysemous vocabulary defaults to the GROUND register: kama = govern (not Mars), maqa = account-mark (not eclipse), kaki = Qollqa / granary (not Pleiades), mama = principal item (not Moon), taki = ritual chant (not Saturn), wama = proper name or supplier identifier (not constellation).

evidence.test: Supporting astronomical marker count: 0/3 (paka, maki, chaki all absent)

v3_contribution.claim: PHYSICAL EVIDENCE DECIDES: UR284's opening is LK tassel (2.5 cm) + needlework bundle (2.0 cm). This construction is IDENTICAL to UR268 (Incahuasi peanut tier-3 district summary, a finished-for-upward-delivery tribute document). The physical convention is TRIBUTE-PRESENTATION, not astronomical-observation.

evidence.test: Physical-opening convention match across UR284 and UR268

v3_contribution.claim: v3 unlocked 13 dictionary-confirmed readings (v1 had 0) over a 22-cord document. The syllabary glosses are stable across the astronomical and commodity interpretations -- only the CONTEXTUAL GLOSS shifts. One reading (p3 mama) recovered by Pass 3A Ascher constraint, flagged [3A] for lower epistemic weight.

evidence.test: STRING readings v1 to v3: 0 to 13

v3_contribution.claim: 3/3 Ascher sums verified (100 percent integrity) on a small 22-cord scale. Audit-grade integrity consistent with a finished tribute ticket, not a field observation notebook (which would have no arithmetic layer).

PAQA-TAQA-KAQA cadastral-anchoring formula at p11-p15 (east-boundary-wall) mirrors UR275 (UE 19 peanut audit), UR277 (UE 17 cadastral map), and UR267A (UE 16 aji day-book). The formula is the Incahuasi-network CADASTRAL ANCHOR convention, not an observation-site pointer.

PRIOR HYPOTHESIS SUPERSEDED: the classifier read UR284 as a compact astronomical observation (Mars + Moon + eclipse + Pleiades + falcon-constellation). That reading is internally consistent at the syllabary level but FAILS the suppression rule and is physically inconsistent with the UR268-twin presentation binding. Documented and retained; superseded by the tribute-ticket reading.

The prior dating attempt (astronomical retrocalculation yielding tentative 1494 or 1496 CE) is DISCARDED under the reclassification. UR284 has no date signature under commodity interpretation; the numerical cords 18, 21, 22 (57, 46, 50) are closing tallies, not astronomical anchors.

companions.note: Incahuasi UE 18 peanut tier-3 district summary. PHYSICAL TWIN: LK tassel + needlework bundle opening IDENTICAL between UR268 and UR284. UR268 is the Incahuasi canonical tribute-presentation tier; UR284 is the Armatambo coastal equivalent.

evidence.test: LK tassel + needlework bundle opening convention match

companions.note: Incahuasi UE 22 audit ledger (BOTH UR272 AND UR284 were initially mis-classified as astronomical and both RECLASSIFIED to commodity_accounting under the same sky/ground polysemy suppression rule). Two parallel cases of the vocabulary trap.

Incahuasi UE 16 commodity production register (26x9 matrix). THIRD khipu in the 'reclassified-from-astronomical' cluster: UR278, UR272, and UR284 all carry polysemous sky-vocabulary in a ground-register context, all three revised to commodity_accounting.
UE 19 peanut audit (47-constant, 5/5 Ascher). Structural parallel for the paqa-taqa-kaqa cadastral-anchoring formula that UR284 uses at p11-p15.

UE 16 aji tier-1 day-book (15-constant). UR284's kama + maqa + taki vocabulary cluster parallels the day-book-tier commodity register (kama as governance-code, maqa as tally-mark, taki as ritual-formula).
Pisco kuraka annual report with kama x15 always bound to S=7 governance code. UR284's kama x2 reading as 'govern' (not Mars) rests directly on UR144's governance-code convention.

word_reading.note: 13/13 dictionary-confirmed STRING readings (100 percent after KFG merge). The syllabary readings are STABLE across the astronomical and commodity interpretations -- only the GLOSS CONTEXT shifts. One [3A] Ascher-recovered reading (p3 mama) carries low epistemic weight in either reading.

document_type.note: RECLASSIFIED to commodity_accounting (tribute presentation ticket) under Phase 3e suppression rule + UR268-twin physical binding evidence. Specific commodity unresolved (no Urton-Chu positional constant fires on the 22-cord document). Sub-type: Armatambo coastal tribute ticket with cadastral anchoring.

narrative.note: Internally coherent under commodity reading (kama governance thesis -> tally tick -> principal item -> phase separator -> supplier/responsibility marker -> delivery chant -> seven count -> first account-mark -> Falcon supplier -> cadastral anchoring east-boundary-wall -> governance returns -> second account-mark -> Qollqa granary -> closing tallies). Externally anchored by UR268 presentation-binding twin and UR275/UR277 cadastral-anchoring formula. The prior astronomical reading is documented and superseded.

data_integrity.note: 3/3 Ascher verified (100 percent). Physical binding preserved (OKR-attested). The reclassification does not affect the knot-level data; it reframes the gloss context. Data quality unchanged at high.

sections.title: Governance Opening Triplet
sections.reading: Governance declaration at p1 (kama, thesis -- NOT Mars), tally-tick at p2 (val=10), principal-item at p3 (mama [3A Ascher-recovered] -- NOT Moon). The commodity-register opening establishes the tribute's governance code, the audit-tick convention, and the class of item being delivered.

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD -- GOVERN (administrator verb; parallel to UR144 kama=7 governance-code)
principal item (not Moon); Ascher-recovered = low epistemic weight

sections.title: Phase Separator
sections.reading: Empty cord -- phase break between the opening declaration and the tribute ledger.

key_cords.note: phase-separator between opening triplet and main ledger

sections.title: Responsibility + Delivery Ceremony
sections.reading: Responsibility-first-person marker (1OBJ-pi at p5, 8 S-knots). taki at p6 = ritual delivery-chant (the tribute-ceremony formula, same register as UR270's liturgical zone). First account-mark (maqa) at p8 on BG identity-color = posted against a named party. Supplier identifier (wama = Falcon, likely proper name) at p9.

key_cords.note: responsibility-marker: 'me-who' for the administrator or supplier
RITUAL DELIVERY CHANT (not Saturn) -- tribute ceremony formula
FIRST ACCOUNT-MARK on identity-color -- posted to a named party
FALCON -- proper name / supplier identifier (not constellation)

sections.title: Cadastral Anchoring Formula
sections.reading: MB/BG phase shift for the paqa-taqa-kaqa cadastral triplet: east (p11), boundary (p12), wall (p15). The tribute is anchored to a SPECIFIC WALLED ENCLOSURE FACING EAST -- the same convention UR275 (UE 19 peanut audit) and UR277 (UE 17 cadastral map) use for their terrestrial anchors.

key_cords.note: wall -- tribute-enclosure completes the paqa-taqa-kaqa triplet

sections.title: Second Governance + Qollqa
sections.reading: Governance returns (kama on BG identity-color at p16). Second account-mark (maqa at p17). DIR-ka direction suffix isolated at p19. kaki at p20 = Qollqa / granary category (NOT Pleiades) -- the final vocabulary cord names the storage category.

key_cords.note: governance returns on identity-color -- named governance act
QOLLQA / granary (commodity-context, not Pleiades) -- storage category of the tribute

sections.title: Closing Tallies
sections.reading: Two pure numerical cords: 46 on MB, 50 on BG. Closing tribute tallies. The document's presentation binding + closing tallies complete the tribute ticket.
